conclusion: Contrary to theoretical predictions suggesting the stability of a high-$T_{c}$ face-centered cubic (fcc) $YH_{10}$ phase, the authors conclude that $YH_{10}$ does not form in the pressure-temperature range up to 410 GPa and 2250 K. Instead, the authors establish that the $P6_3/mmc$ $YH_{9}$ phase remains the hydrogen-richest yttrium hydride stable under these conditions.
